content:
- The schema-mismatch long-lane slice exposed a real defect: bounded failure state is persisted, but the runner emits no operator-visible stderr context for the reconcile failure.
- Created `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-schema-mismatch-reconcile-failure-missing-operator-stderr.md` to track the defect and ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` so Ralph can switch work instead of marking story 23 task 01 as passed with an untracked issue.
- Next: pick up the new bug task and fix logging via red-green TDD; do not mark the original audit task passed until the bug is resolved or otherwise accounted for by the task system.

- Read previous progress and confirmed the last context created a new unblocked bug task for missing operator stderr on schema-mismatch reconcile failure.
- Read current active and done task summaries; active backlog currently contains one unblocked failing high-priority bug, two other failing tasks, and one blocked failing task.
- Next I am refreshing the Ralph summaries, checking the chooser rules file, and confirming the selected task path before writing .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-read the chooser rules plus the selected bug task definition.
- Confirmed task selection precedence still points to the unblocked failing high-priority bug `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-schema-mismatch-reconcile-failure-missing-operator-stderr.md`; story 26 remains blocked by unfinished story 23 task 01.
- Next context should execute that bug via red-green TDD, then run make check, make test, and make lint before marking anything passed.

- Read previous codex progress entries and confirmed the active task path is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-schema-mismatch-reconcile-failure-missing-operator-stderr.md`.
- Re-read the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skills from disk so this turn follows the Ralph workflow correctly.
- Next: inspect the task file contents, determine whether it ends in TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE, then either create/update a plan and quit or start the red-green implementation loop.

- Read the bug task markdown and confirmed it has no `<plan>` pointer and no execution marker, so this turn must produce a planning artifact and then stop.
- Inspected the current long-lane and support changes around schema mismatch: the long-lane test now expects stderr evidence, `SchemaMismatchAudit` already asserts stderr plus persisted `last_error`, and the harness captures runner stderr snapshots.
- Inspected the runtime/logging boundary: `runner::execute` emits a startup event, but `main.rs` only writes callback events when log format is JSON, and `reconcile_runtime` records apply failures in tracking state/metrics without emitting an operator event while the runner stays alive.
- Next: add a bug-task plan file plus `<plan>` pointer in the task markdown, ending the plan with `TO BE VERIFIED`, then quit immediately per Ralph workflow.

- Created the planning artifact `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-schema-mismatch-reconcile-failure-missing-operator-stderr_plans/2026-04-20-reconcile-failure-stderr-plan.md` for the active bug task and added the matching `<plan>` pointer to the task markdown.
- The plan captures the actual boundary problem discovered from source inspection: reconcile failures are persisted and metrics-recorded, but no live operator event is emitted for the bounded failure path, and `main.rs` currently suppresses callback events in text mode.
- Plan ends in `TO BE VERIFIED` as required for a planning-only turn. No code execution or test lanes were run in this context.
- Next context should review the plan, decide whether the event-sink design is correct, replace the plan terminator with `NOW EXECUTE` when satisfied, and then quit immediately per Ralph workflow.

- Read previous progress, the active bug task pointer, the bug task markdown, and re-read the required tdd + improve-code-boundaries skill files from disk.
- Confirmed the task already has a planning artifact, so this turn is deciding whether that plan remains TO BE VERIFIED or is ready to flip to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next: inspect the plan contents carefully, verify the boundary and TDD design still look correct against the current codebase, then either update the plan marker and quit immediately or leave it as verification-needed and quit.

- Verified the current runtime boundary against source: `main.rs` still suppresses callback events in text mode, while `reconcile_runtime` persists bounded apply failures without emitting any live operator event.
- Confirmed `operator-log` already supports both text and JSON formatting for a single `LogEvent`, so the plan does not require a broader logging redesign.
- Next: inspect the failure shape one more time for whether existing typed data can support a clean event helper; if yes, flip the plan terminator to `NOW EXECUTE` and stop immediately.

- Re-verified the plan against current source boundaries: text-mode runtime events are still suppressed in `main.rs`, reconcile failures are still only persisted in `reconcile_runtime`, and `operator-log` already supports the needed text/json parity.
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-schema-mismatch-reconcile-failure-missing-operator-stderr_plans/2026-04-20-reconcile-failure-stderr-plan.md` to replace the stale planning assumption and switch the terminator from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: quit immediately now; the next context should read the task and verified plan, then execute the red-green slices exactly as written, run the required check/test lanes, and only mark the task passed after all gates are green.

- Read Ralph progress, confirmed active task `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-schema-mismatch-reconcile-failure-missing-operator-stderr.md`, and re-read the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill files from disk.
- Confirmed the previous context already flipped the bug plan to `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn should execute the plan rather than create a new one.
- Next: read the task markdown and the referenced plan in full, then implement the fix via red-green slices and run `make check` plus `make test` before marking the task passed.

- Read the active bug task and its verified execution plan; the plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`, so this context is in implementation mode.
- The execution contract is to restore operator-visible stderr for bounded reconcile failures without regressing `last_error` persistence or bounded retry behavior.
- Next: inspect the current tests and runtime event path, run the first smallest red test, then patch only enough code to make that slice green before moving on.

- Ran the first real red slice: `cargo test -p runner ignored_long_lane_classifies_customer_schema_mismatch_through_a_typed_audit --test default_bootstrap_long_lane -- --ignored --exact`.
- The test failed exactly on the intended public behavior boundary: `SchemaMismatchAudit` found persisted failure state but empty runner stderr, proving the bug is still real.
- Next: implement the smallest honest event path so bounded reconcile failures emit runtime events and text-mode `main.rs` stops discarding them, then rerun this same long-lane test before moving to wider checks.

- Patched the runtime event boundary so `main.rs` now writes emitted runtime events in text mode too, and `runner::execute` now threads a shared event sink into the reconcile runtime.
- Added a typed reconcile apply-failure operator event in `crates/runner/src/reconcile_runtime/mod.rs`; it emits after rollback plus persisted failure tracking, preserving the bounded failure semantics.
- Re-ran the original red slice `cargo test -p runner ignored_long_lane_classifies_customer_schema_mismatch_through_a_typed_audit --test default_bootstrap_long_lane -- --ignored --exact`, and it is now green.
- Next: run the required repo gates `make check` and `make test`, then do a final boundary review and update the task/plan files only after all required checks pass.

- `make check` passed cleanly and the targeted ignored schema-mismatch long lane stayed green.
- `make test` failed on `run_reconciles_each_mapping_into_only_its_own_tables_inside_a_shared_destination_database`, but the failure was a webhook bind race (`failed to bind webhook listener`), not the schema-mismatch stderr path.
- Next: reproduce that reconcile-contract failure in isolation to determine whether it is a flaky port-allocation issue or a regression introduced by the runtime event changes; only then decide whether code changes are required or a rerun is sufficient.

- Required gates are now green: `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all passed, and the targeted ignored schema-mismatch long lane was already verified separately.
- A transient webhook bind race occurred during the first `make test` run but passed in isolated rerun and full-suite rerun; I am treating that as a separate flaky-test bug instead of ignoring it.
- Next: do the final boundary review, file the flaky port-allocation bug task, then update the active bug task metadata, switch Ralph task state, commit, and push.
